mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 36-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases over a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profits and positive monthly equity returns during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during recessionary periods or when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 195 signal persistent inflationary pressure, prompting market pricing of tighter financial conditions and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold over a 24-month sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of cases over a 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion, which systematically supports corporate earnings and yields positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDP growth and forward returns remains below 0.05 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and recessionary fears, triggering rapid risk-off selling and negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative forward returns occur in <40% of triggered instances during contraction phases over a 3-year walk-forward test.
